original: Space probe Voyager 1 takes a photograph of the entire solar system, the images transmitted in real-time with earth a 'pale blue dot'
American sprinter Andre Cason runs world record 60m indoor (6.41s) in Madrid, Spain

Britain'sJayne TorvillandChristopher Deanfamously dominate ice dancing at the Sarajevo Winter Olympics, performing toMaurice Ravel's "Bolero" in their free dance routine and recording 9-of-9 perfect scores for artistic impression
Alexandre Bilodeauwins the gold medal in the freestyle skiing men's moguls event, becoming the first Canadian to win gold during an Olympic Games hosted in Canada
American snowboarderShaun Whitewins an unprecedented 3rd Olympic halfpipe gold medal (previous 2006, 10) at the Pyeongchang Winter Games
Luna 20 (Russia) launched to orbit & soft landing on Moon
The first of 24 satellites of the Global Positioning System is placed in orbit
Online video-sharing site YouTube is founded in San Mateo, California, by Steve Chen, Chad Hurley, and Jawed Karim
Margaret E. Knight, American inventor (flat-bottomed paper bag), described as a "female Thomas Edison", born in York, Maine (d. 1914)
George Washington Gale Ferris Jr., American engineer and inventor (Ferris Wheel), born in Galesburg, Illinois (d. 1896)
Herbert A. Hauptman, American mathematician whose models for determining the chemical crystal structures vital for life revolutionized chemistry (Nobel 1985), born in New York City (d. 2011)
Venus is both a morning star & evening star
First "micro on a chip" or microchip is patented by Texas Instruments
Kieren John Perkins swims world record 800m freestyle (7:46.60)
Spacecraft NEAR Shoemaker enters orbit around asteroid 433 Eros, the first spacecraft to orbit an asteroid
Aretha Franklinrecords her single "Respect" at Atlantic Studio in NYC; her rendition ofOtis Redding's tune becomes Billboard Song of the Year (1967)
Comedy film "Wayne's World" starringMike Myersand Dana Carvey, based on their Saturday Night Live sketch premieres
US launches Solar Maximum Mission Observatory to study solar flares
The Large Hadron Collider (LHC), the world's largest and highest-energy particle accelerator, will be switched off for two years for upgrading
AstronomerJames Bradleypresents his discovery of the wobbling motion of the Earth on its axis to the Royal Society, London
The original "Dracula" film starringBela Lugosias the titular vampire is released
Wilt Chamberlainbreaks NBA career scoring record at 20,884 points
Muhammad Alibeats British heavyweight boxing champion Joe Bugner by unanimous points decision in 12 rounds at Las Vegas Convention Centre
Soviet cross-country skierNikolay Zimyatovwins first of 3 gold medals at the Lake Placid Winter Olympics when he takes out 30k event; also wins relay and 50k gold
Julius Nieuwland, Belgian-American chemist (inventor of 1st synthetic rubber neoprene), born in Hansbeke, Belgium (d. 1936)
Willem J. Kolff, Dutch-American Physician and Biomedical Engineer (artificial kidney, hemodialysis), born in Leiden, Netherlands (d. 2009)
Takuo Aoyagi, Japanese engineer who invented the pulse oximeter, born in Niigata Prefecture, Japan (d. 2020)
Pham Tuan, Vietnamese fighter pilot and the first Vietnamese cosmonaut (Soyuz 37/36), born in Kiến Xương, Vietnam
US launches communications satellite Syncom 1

Element 103, Lawrencium, is first produced in Berkeley, California, by bombarding a three-milligram target of three isotopes of californium with boron-10 and boron-11 nuclei from the Heavy Ion Linear Accelerator

Esther Morris appointed US' first female in Justice of the Peace in South Pass City, Wyoming, after previous justice, R.S. Barr, resigned to protest passage of Wyoming Territory's women's suffrage amendment in 1869
Heidemarie Rosendahl, German athlete (Olympic gold FRG long jump, 4×100m relay, silver pentathlon 1972), born in Hückeswagen, Germany
John C. Frémontand his party become the first Europeans to discover Lake Tahoe during his second expedition through the American West
Otis Tufts, American machinist and inventor who built printing machines, steam engines, firefighting equipment and invented the steam pile driver, born in Cambridge, Massachusetts (d. 1869)
Fritz Zwicky, Swiss astronomer (supernova, dark matter), born in Varna, Bulgaria (d. 1974)
Seraph Young becomes the first woman to legally vote in the modern United States, two days after the Utah legislature passed a law allowing women the vote
Fictional character Skeezix is discovered on the Wallet family's doorstep in Frank King's cartoon strip "Gasoline Alley"
Norwegian 15k Olympic cross country champion Harald Grønningen wins his second gold medal of the Grenoble Winter Olympics in the 4 x 10k relay
Akbar, aged 13, is crowned the 3rd Mughal Emperor, succeeding his late father Humayun at Kalanaur in the Punjab
Southern Christian Leadership Conference organizes in New Orleans withMartin Luther King Jr.as its President
Portland Trail Blazers tradeClyde Drexlerto the Houston Rockets, who end up winning the 1995 NBA Championship
Juan Pujol Garcia, Spanish double agent (spied for Britain, codename Garbo, pretended to spy for Nazi Germany codename Arabel), born in Barcelona, Spain (d. 1988)
Banjo Matthews, American auto racing constructor (72% of winning cars in top NASCAR division 1974-85; 262 wins in 362 Cup races), born in Akron, Ohio (d. 1996)
Madhubala [Mumtaz Jehan Begum Dehlavi], Indian actress regarded as "The Venus of Indian Cinema", born in Delhi, India (d. 1969)
Ricardo Rodríguez, Mexican auto racer (youngest F1 driver ever to race for Ferrari), born in Mexico City (d. 1962)
Maceo Parker, American tenor saxophonist (James Brown; Parliament-Funkadelic, King's One, Mo' Roots), born in Kinston, North Carolina
Gregory Hines, American actor and dancer (White Nights, Taps), born in New York City (d. 2003)
Moroney scores cricket twin centuries for Australia at Johannesburg
British pop music duo Peter & Gordon release single "A World Without Love" (written byPaul McCartney, credited to Lennon-McCartney)
1st US state bird refuge authorized (Lake Merritt, California)
Detroit Pistons-Philadelphia 76ers game draws crowd of 53,745 at Pontiac Silverdome, Pontiac; third largest crowd in NBA history; Pistons win, 125-107
"Whitney Houston" debut album by Whitney Houston is released (Grammy Award Best Pop Vocal Performance, Female 1986, Billboard Album of Year 1986)

Battle of Cape St. Vincent: British fleet under Admiral Sir John Jervis defeats larger Spanish fleet under Admiral Don José de Córdoba y Ramos near Cape St. Vincent, Portugal. CaptainHoratio Nelsondistinguishes himself.
Richard Allen, 1st African American ordained by Methodist-Episcopal church, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1831)
Charles Wilson, Scottish physicist and meteorologist (Wilson cloud chamber-Nobel), born in Glencorse, Scotland (d. 1959)
Aida Overton Walker, African-American dancer and singer (In Dahomey; Salome), born in Richmond, Virginia (d. 1914)
Charles Oatley, English electrical engineer (developed scanning electron microscope), born in From, England (d. 1996)
Lois Maxwell [Hooker], Canadian actress (Goldfinger, Dr. No, Miss Moneypenny), born in Kitchener, Ontario (d. 2007)

Bernie "Boom Boom" Geoffrion, Canadian Hockey HOF right wing (11 x NHL All-star; 6 x Stanley Cup - Montreal Canadiens, New York Rangers) and coach (NY Rangers, Atlanta Flames, Montreal Canadiens), born in Montreal, Quebec (d. 2001)
Florence Henderson, American actress (The Brady Bunch - "Carol"), and singer, born in Dale, Indiana (d. 2016)
